questions.question: What is the value of a digit in the thousands place?
questions.explanation: Place value is determined by multiplying the digit by the value of its position.

questions.question: How many periods are there in the Indian number system?
questions.explanation: The periods include ones, thousands, lakhs, and crores.

questions.question: Where is the first comma placed in the Indian number system?
questions.correct_answer: After three digits from the right
questions.explanation: Commas are used to separate groups of two or more digits.

questions.question: What is the value of a digit in the ones place?
questions.explanation: Place value is determined by multiplying the digit by the value of its position.

questions.question: How many lakhs are there in one crore?
questions.explanation: Lakhs and crores are used to represent large numbers.

questions.question: What is the value of a digit in the tens place?
questions.explanation: Place value is determined by multiplying the digit by the value of its position.

questions.question: What is the difference between a lakh and a crores?
questions.explanation: Lakhs and crores are used to represent large numbers.

questions.question: How many periods are there in the decimal system?
questions.explanation: The decimal system has only one period, the ones place.

questions.question: What is the value of a digit in the hundreds place?
questions.explanation: Place value is determined by multiplying the digit by the value of its position.

questions.question: What is the difference between a thousand and a lakh?
questions.correct_answer: A lakh has one hundred thousand, not a thousand
questions.explanation: Lakhs and thousands are used to represent large numbers.

notes.title: Indian Number System Periods
notes.content: The Indian number system has four periods: Ones (up to hundreds), Thousands (thousands and ten thousands), Lakhs (lakhs and ten lakhs), and Crores (crores and ten crores).

notes.title: Place Value
notes.content: Place value is determined by multiplying the digit by the value of its position.

notes.title: Commas in Indian Number System
notes.content: Commas are used to separate groups of two or more digits, except for the first comma which comes after three digits.

notes.title: Decimal System Periods
notes.content: The decimal system has only one period, the ones place.

facts.fact: In the Indian system, commas are placed after every two digits from the right, except the first comma which comes after three digits.
The Indian number system has been used for thousands of years and is still widely used today.
Commas are an important part of the Indian number system, as they help to separate groups of two or more digits.
A lakh has one hundred thousand, not a thousand.
